channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Hackers use GenAI to attack more frequently and effectively
item.description: Generative AI is being used in cybersecurity solutions and threats, report claims - so how can your business cope?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Don’t let this credit card fraud nightmare happen to you
item.description: Over 400,000 cases of credit card fraud were reported in 2024 to the Federal Trade Commission. The CyberGuy explains how to protect against becoming another victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: CASSIS, France (AP) — In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 leak gives us another hint about one of its key specs
item.description: Matching up with previous rumors, a new leak suggests the Exynos 2500 will power the Galaxy Z Flip 7.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DOJ indicts North Korean conspirators for remote IT work scheme
item.description: The US Department of Justice this week announced that it had indicted two North Korean nationals and three other men, accusing them of participating in a conspiracy designed to trick US companies into funding the North Korean regime.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Last 4 escaped monkeys are captured in South Carolina after months on the loose
item.description: Officials say they have recaptured the final four monkeys who escaped two months ago from a South Carolina compound that breeds them for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: People are hawking TikTok-loaded phones for thousands on eBay, Facebook
item.description: People are listing phones preloaded with TikTok for tens of thousands of dollars on eBay, Facebook marketplace and other online storefronts — though it is not clear if there are many buyers at those prices. TikTok was briefly unavailable to U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Linkedin sued for training AI on users’ private messages
item.description: A lawsuit in California accuses Linkedin of using private messages on its platform to train AI models, according to the BBC.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The OpenJDK’s plans for Java in 2025
item.description: Java enhancement plans for 2025 range from improving performance in the foreign function and memory (FFM) API, to working on ahead-of-time (AOT) code compilation, to finalizing the structured concurrency API, according to a recently published presentation from the OpenJDK community.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Spain's leader wants the EU to ‘make social media great again.’ Here's how
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez is launching a crusade against what he sees as the downsides of social media.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How to cope when disasters strip away photos, heirlooms and other pieces of the past
item.description: They’re the possessions that tell your story: the photos of old friends and relatives. The ring your mom left you. The hand-knit Christmas stockings. Your grandfather's secretary desk and the letters inside.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Stratoshark analyzes cloud applications at a syscall level
item.description: Securing cloud applications is both easy and hard. Easy because hyperscale providers such as Microsoft Azure are focused on ensuring that tenant compute environments are locked down and isolated from each other.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Prosecutors charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset with the federal TikTok ban with multiple counts on Wednesday, including arson.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Tech titans sound off on Trump's AI project
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 best home inventory apps to protect your property in case of emergency
item.description: Home inventory and restoration apps help itemize and track every item in your home in the event you have to make an insurance claim after a natural disaster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A new security seal of approval is coming to your smart home gadgets
item.description: The White House launched a new cybersecurity safety label, the U.S. Cyber Trust Mark, intended to help consumers make informed decisions on smart device safety.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Copilot AI comes to Microsoft 365 plans: Everything you need to know
item.description: If you’re a Microsoft 365 subscriber with a Personal or Family subscription, Microsoft just flipped a switch and activated Copilot AI features for your account. It’s a new part of your subscription, along with that 1TB of OneDrive storage and access to Office apps like Word and Excel.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to deal with a Big Pile of Mud
item.description: Last week I talked about where those big, unwieldy code bases come from. They don’t appear out of nowhere, and no one ever plans to create one.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: State of JavaScript: Highlights of the JavaScript developer survey
item.description: Getting a complete picture of the multi-headed beast that is the JavaScript ecosystem isn’t easy, but the annual State of JavaScript survey is a good place to start. What makes this survey stand out is that it attracts the input of thousands of working developers and other JavaScript enthusiasts.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle lead $500B Project Stargate to ramp up AI infra in the US
item.description: Several large technology firms including OpenAI, SoftBank, Oracle, Nvidia, and MGX have partnered to set up a new company in the US to ramp up AI infrastructure in the country.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sustainability, grid demands, AI workloads will challenge data center growth in 2025
item.description: Data centers this year will face several challenges as the demand for artificial intelligence introduces an evolution in AI hardware, on-premises and cloud-based strategies for training and inference, and innovations in power distributions—all while opposition to new data center developments continu
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Does your Windows 11 PC keep restarting? Let's fix that frustrating problem
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers Windows 11 restart fixes: Update drivers, run system scans and check hardware.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The bitter lesson for generative AI adoption
item.description: The rapid development and improvement in generative AI technology pose a challenge for training and fine-tuning as a sustainable path for adoption. If organizations constantly need to fine-tune new models for specific tasks, they might be in a costly cycle of catching up with new technology.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How to manage photos, videos on new laptop with limited space
item.description: With limited hard drive space, tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers these tips for storing large video files and photos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
